title: Identifying the most important predictors of support for climate policy in the United States
authors: Matthew H. Goldberg, Abel Gustafson, Matthew T. Ballew, Seth A. Rosenthal, Anthony Leiserowitz

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: Regulate carbon dioxide (the primary greenhouse gas) as a pollutant
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: This is an emissions regulation, not a tax instrument.

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: Require electric utilities to produce at least 20% of their electricity from wind, solar, or other renewable energy sources
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: This is a renewable electricity standard, not a carbon or fuel tax.

inclusion_reason: The paper studies climate policy support generally, but it does not analyze any carbon tax or fuel tax instrument.
exclusion_reason: No direct carbon tax, CO2 tax, fuel tax, gasoline tax, or equivalent fuel excise policy is analyzed.
